_doc.purpose: Declare the options-chain refresh universe explicitly, with an asserted expected count, so that a SILENTLY SHRINKING universe is distinguishable from a healthy run.
why_this_exists: Before W6B-11 the universe was an argparse default (['SPY']) inside
chad/market_data/options_chain_refresh.py. The live unit invokes the module
with no --symbols argument, so that hardcoded default WAS the production
The health rule R17 already catches two failure shapes: an empty chains map,
and a non-empty `error` field (which the refresher sets for any symbol it
ATTEMPTED and failed). What neither R17 nor the refresher caught is a symbol
that is never attempted at all -- if the universe shrinks, chains is smaller
but non-empty, no error is set, the unit exits 0, and every downstream check
reports green while coverage has quietly dropped.
Operator ruling 2026-07-24 (D6): SPY-only is CONFIRMED correct. This file
does not change the universe; it declares the universe that already exists
and makes any future drift from it loud.
expected_count_is_deliberately_redundant: `symbols` and `expected_count` restate the same fact on purpose. Editing one
without the other is a contradiction the refresher REFUSES to run on. That is
what makes 'exit 0 with 1 symbol' permanently distinguishable from 'exit 0
with nothing': emptying the symbol list can no longer silently become a
zero-coverage green run -- it becomes a loud config error.
how_to_change_the_universe: Update `symbols` AND `expected_count` together, in a single Pending Action.
Never lower expected_count to silence a coverage alert -- a shortfall means
the fetch failed, not that the expectation was wrong.
_doc.fallback: If this file is absent, unreadable, or malformed, the refresher falls back to its historical ['SPY'] default and logs the fallback. Absence must never harden into an outage.
